extra.reasoning: The property requires that setting syncope=true (with all other inputs unchanged) never lowers acuity. I examined all 10 rules (r1-r10). Their conditions reference only: dizziness (r1), pallor (r2), palpitations (r3), rash (r4), back_pain (r5), severe_headache (r6), confusion (r7), vision_loss (r8), fever (r9), and dyspnea (r10). None of the rules references the input variable syncope. The default (acuity := routine) and the priority/order conflict-resolution mechanism likewise do not depend on syncope. Therefore acuity is completely invariant to the value of syncope: for any fixed assignment of the other 10 inputs, the computed acuity with syncope=false is identical to the acuity with syncope=true. Since the value is unchanged (equal), it is trivially "no lower than before." The monotonicity property holds for the entire input space.
